name: Royal Hua Hin Golf Course
notes: Thailand's oldest golf course and the first in the Kingdom — established 1924 during the reign of King Rama VII. Construction actually began in 1919 under King Rama VI (first 9 holes); the full 18 holes were completed in 1928. Designer A.O. Robins was a Scottish railway engineer serving as Phetchaburi Railway Officer. Course is located in central Hua Hin adjacent to the famous Hua Hin Railway Station. 6,678 yards from white tees, 5,713 yards from red tees. Features limestone cliff outcrops and mature tropical trees — a traditional British-style parkland layout. Hosts major events including Singha Thailand Junior World Golf Championship and Singha-SAT Thai LPGA Masters. Celebrated its 100th anniversary in 2024. Course condition described as 'not in great condition but still very playable' by some reviewers. Green fee rates vary by source: huahingolfcourse.com lists THB 1,300 flat; golfsavers.com lists weekday THB 1,000 / weekend THB 1,350; 1golf.eu lists THB 2,000. Weekend/weekday split from golfsavers.com used here as most granular. Also listed as managed by Santiburi at santiburi.com/huahin. Booking email: royalgolfhuahin.booking@gmail.com. GPS from golfhuahin.com: 12.56771, 99.95401.
